title: Shade Screens
desc: The lookout slit above the rift needs its shade screens reset before the afternoon glare turns every lower movement into a false charge.

steps.text:
At one turn of the upper wall, the patrol route widens into a slit-post where UnderWorld lookouts study the lower basin through a pair of narrow shade screens and a stone sight shelf cut directly into the rock. The station works best when light is controlled ruthlessly. Too much glare off the mineral seams below and every shifting reflection begins looking like motion with intent. Today's noon light has already made a liar of the post twice.

The slit keeper shades her eyes with one hand and scowls into the rift. "Reset the sliding screens if you want the shutters," she says. "Or clean the sight shelf and measuring notches if you prefer the station itself. I'd rather not file another report that turns out to be sunlight behaving like an ambush."

options.label: Reset the sliding shade screens over the lookout slit
Clean the sight shelf and refresh the measuring notches

steps.text:
The shade screens ride in narrow stone grooves and can be drawn by degrees to cut the glare without sacrificing the lower view entirely. Two have jammed half-open and a third sits crooked enough to throw an uneven bar of light across the whole shelf. You free the track, square the panels, and slide each screen back to the keeper's marks until the slit admits only the useful part of the day.

The effect is immediate. What had been scattered brightness below resolves into ledges, rubble fans, and the occasional drifting vapor thread that no longer resembles a charging beast unless one badly wants it to. The keeper rests both elbows on the shelf and watches the basin settle into honesty. "Better. Fear Valley invents enough threats without help from the sun."

options.label: Latch the screens and leave the slit reading true

steps.text:
The sight shelf holds the station together: elbow rest, line notches, bearing cuts, and a set of shallow depth marks every lookout uses whether they trust them or not. A film of dust and ash has dulled the notches until distance judgments have started sounding more poetic than military. You brush the shelf clean, clear the measuring cuts with a narrow pick, and redraw the two faded marks most used for the lower approach line.

When the keeper sights across the restored shelf, her posture changes at once from irritated to professional. The valley below remains hostile, but it stops pretending to be abstract. "Good," she says. "If something comes out of that basin, I would like to describe it precisely before I dislike it."

options.label: Set the pick aside and leave the shelf legible again
